name: Quang Noodles

ingredients.item: water
pumpkin, peeled and cut into cubes
chayote or kohlrabi, peeled and half cubed and half diced
taro, diced
salt, divided
cold-pressed peanut or vegetable oil, divided
block (14 ounces/400 g) firm tofu, cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) thick steaks
finger-size pieces fresh turmeric (see Note), peeled and roughly chopped
leek (white and green parts separated), roughly chopped, divided
mushrooms (straw, shiitake, or similar), cut into bite-size pieces
store-bought or homemade seitan, sliced
vegetable or mushroom stock powder
sugar
freshly ground black pepper
dried flat rice noodles or 2¼ pounds (1 kg) fresh mì Quảng or flat rice noodles
leaf or iceberg lettuce, shredded or torn
fresh green herbs (such as mint, perilla, cilantro, and/or Asian basil leaves), chopped
sesame rice crackers
cilantro, chopped, for garnish
crushed roasted peanuts, for garnish
limes, cut into wedges, for serving
whole green serrano chili peppers. for serving
banana blossom, prepped and shredded

method: Add the water to a medium saucepan and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Add pumpkin and chayote cubes, cook until fork-tender (about 15 minutes), and then remove from heat. Purée the mixture with a hand blender. Add diced chayote and taro to saucepan with 1 teaspoon of the salt and cook over medium heat until fork-tender (about 7 minutes).

Heat ¼ cup (60 ml) of the oil in a small skillet over medium-low heat. When hot, add tofu steaks and cook until golden brown on all sides (10 to 15 minutes). Transfer to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.
If using fresh turmeric, place it in a resealable plastic bag and crush it with a pestle (avoid using the mortar, as the turmeric may stain it and/or your hands) or meat tenderizer.
In a mortar and pestle, finely crush the white part of the leek with ½ teaspoon of the salt.

In a medium skillet, heat the remaining ¼ cup (60 ml) oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until golden (about 2 minutes). If using fresh turmeric, add and quickly cook it for about 10 seconds.

Add mushrooms, tofu, and seitan to the skillet. Season with turmeric powder (if using), remaining 1 teaspoon salt, stock powder, and sugar. Toss well, cook over low heat until mushrooms are tender (2 to 3 minutes), and then turn off heat. Add green part of leek and ground pepper.
Follow the directions on the package to cook the dried flat rice noodles. If using fresh noodles, quickly blanch them in hot water if they have been refrigerated.

Fill serving bowls halfway with noodles, and then ladle soup over the top until it barely covers them. Add some lettuce and herbs to each bowl. Break a rice cracker into small pieces and add to bowl. Garnish with cilantro, crushed peanuts, and a full piece of sesame rice cracker.
Squeeze lime wedges over the top of the bowls and mix well. Serve with serrano chili peppers, banana blossom, and more lettuce and herbs.
